title: Common 3-letter words
description: Build real-word fluency with the most common English short words
text: the and had has did dad sad fed jak fee see fad lad add age sit ten ape rat
finger_focus: All learned letters — focus on speed

tips.text: These little words do most of the work in any sentence you will ever write. Learn them by hand and your fingers stop spelling and start typing.
A word is one motion, not three. Do not stop between its letters; let it land in a single beat.

tips.target: the and had has did dad sad fed jak
tips.hint: All learned letters - focus on speed

tips.text: Keep your eyes on the copy, not on the keys. Your fingers know where they live; let them prove it.

tips.target: fee see fad lad add age sit ten ape rat
tips.hint: All learned letters - focus on speed

character_tips.anna: These small words are the mortar between the bricks. Type them until they come without thought, and the long ones get easier too. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: A handful of short words fills most of every page. Speed here comes from rhythm, not from pressing harder. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Short words, short reach. Settle into an even beat and they go by quickly enough. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Get friendly with these little words. You will meet them in every line you ever set. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
